entry.title: Washington Post: 23 Jul, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • As Europe’s heat wave melts roads, Tour de France races into an uncertain future
  • Ukraine Live Briefing: Attack on Odessa denounced as ‘outrageous’; 2 Americans die in Donbas region
  • Russia attacks Odessa port a day after signing grain deal, Ukraine says
  • How will the Ukraine grain deal affect the global food crisis?
  • Cuba to hold referendum on same-sex marriage
  • Inside the long, fraught quest to recapture Mexican drug lord Caro Quintero
  • Secret Australian marijuana facility exposes location after turning sky pink
  • After ousting leader, Sri Lanka still staggered by one-two economic punch
  • Ukraine Live Briefing: Grain deal signed in Turkey; U.S. to send Ukraine more artillery
  • As Ukraine pounds Russian targets, U.S. sends more artillery
  • First cases of monkeypox in children in U.S. confirmed
  • Uwe Seeler, German soccer champ, dies at 85
  • Pope's Indigenous tour signals a rethink of mission legacy
  • Greece battles 4 major wildfires; hotels, homes evacuated
  • Iran's state TV says 2 moderate quakes hit southern province
  • Iran says it dismantled group with ties to Israel's Mossad
  • Iraq submits complaint to UN against Turkey after attack
  • UN health agency chief declares monkeypox a global emergency
  • 10 die in northwest China after mountain slope collapses
  • 150,000 people march for LGBTQ rights in Berlin
  • Iran media: Gunmen kill brother of collapsed tower’s owner
  • Pakistan death toll from monsoon rains, flooding reaches 304
  • She ‘fell’ out of a patrol car and died, police say. Her family wants answers.
  • Heat scorches Northeast as temperatures skyrocket to dangerous levels
  • 2 dead, 1 missing after flash floods hit fire-scarred areas of N.M.
  • Biden ‘likely’ has BA.5 coronavirus variant, White House says
  • Steve Bannon found guilty in Jan. 6 contempt of Congress trial
  • Trump’s choices escalated tensions and set U.S. on path to Jan. 6, panel finds
  • Hearings test Trump’s clout and GOP’s wish to ‘forget about Jan. 6’
  • Man accused of posing as federal law enforcement official signals guilty plea
  • In Detroit, the chip shortage has left the city eerily short of cars
  • Big Tech is bracing for a possible recession, spooking other industries
  • Online Schooling Is the Bad Idea That Refuses to Die
  • A Black Family Won Back Its Beach. The Law Remains Broken.
  • Struggling to Stay Cool? So Is the Generator Powering Your Aircon
  • Conservatives Shouldn’t Write Off ‘Trussonomics’ Just Yet
  • Why Europe Has a New Weapon for Bond ‘Fragmentation’
  • The unbearable cruelty of no-exception abortion bans
  • Now we know the truth on what Trump sought to obscure about Jan. 6
  • The Secret Service texting scandal demands answers
  • In the skies over Belarus, a false bomb report signaled danger on the ground
  • The 988 hotline could be transformational — if done right
  • Is 18 minutes enough time for a subject to comment?
  • Ask Eugene Robinson about the latest news around the U.S. and world
  • Chat with Perry Bacon Jr. about politics and beyond
  • Chat with Jennifer Rubin about her columns, politics, policy and more
  • Need a dose of humor in your day? Chat with Alexandra Petri.
Breaking news and analysis on politics, business, world national news, entertainment more. In-depth DC, Virginia, Maryland news coverage including traffic, weather, crime, education, restaurant reviews and more.

entry.content:
Articles in this issue:

entry.title: Sydney Morning Herald: 23 Jul, 2022

entry.content:
Articles in this issue:

Breaking news from Sydney, Australia and the world. Features the latest business, sport, entertainment, travel, lifestyle, and technology news.

entry.content:

SCMP.com, Hong Kong's premier online English daily provides exclusive up-to-date news, audio video news, podcasts, RSS Feeds, Blogs, breaking news, top stories, award winning news and analysis on Hong Kong and China.

entry.title: Asahi Shimbun: 23 Jul, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • High COVID-19 counts in Tokyo, Osaka, Okinawa and other areas
  • Todai to offer metaverse studies to teach engineering
  • Suspect in Abe slaying ordered to undergo psychiatric tests
  • Owner closes 115-year-old ‘soba’ shop because his kids are allergic
  • Kishida wanted rationale for holding state funeral for Abe
  • SKorea to lift ban on NKorea TV, newspapers despite tensions
  • Ukraine and Russia seal grain deal as U.S. pledges more military aid
  • Comic C'moA digital manga site set to build market abroad
  • EDITORIAL: Diplomacy must be a vital key in strengthening Japan’s security
  • VOX POPULI: Living in a world where inflation will be with us for years to come
The Asahi Shimbun is widely regarded for its journalism as the most respected daily newspaper in Japan. The English version offers selected articles from the vernacular Asahi Shimbun, as well as extensive coverage of cool Japan,focusing on manga, travel and other timely news.

entry.title: Korea Herald: 23 Jul, 2022
entry.content:

  • [Temple to Table] Way to live healthier: Bibimbap with eggplant

entry.title: NY Times (Print): 23 Jul, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Russia Agrees to Let Ukraine Ship Grain, Easing World Food Shortage
  • After Roe, Republicans Sharpen Attacks on Gay and Transgender Rights
  • Biden’s New Economic Scorecard: The Price at the Pump
  • How the Government Is Failing Americans Uprooted by Calamity

  • After Mocking France’s Literary Elite, a Fraught Invite Into the Club

  • See How Far Water Levels in Lake Mead Have Fallen

  • Newsom Raises His Profile With Hardball Tactics, Starting With a Gun Bill
  • ‘Cannabis!’ Review: Preaching to the Partaking Choir
  • Review: A New Opera Tells an Original Story With an Open Heart
  • Twitter’s earnings falter as it fights with Elon Musk.
  • A Recession Alarm Is Ringing on Wall Street
  • N.B.A. Mostly Keeps Low Profile in Public Campaign to Free Brittney Griner
  • Fighting a Brutal Regime With the Help of a Video Game
  • Germany bails out Uniper, a crucial gas provider squeezed by Russia.
  • T-Mobile Reaches $500 Million Settlement in Huge 2021 Data Breach
  • Bucking the global trend, Russia lowers interest rates again.
  • Jay Carney, Amazon’s Top Policy Executive, Will Join Airbnb
  • Vince McMahon retires from W.W.E. after stepping down amid a board investigation.
  • How to Lower Your Summer Electric Bill
  • Executors of Jeffrey Epstein’s estate are accused of moving $13 million.
  • Herbert Diess, Volkswagen’s C.E.O., is stepping down.
  • The Myth of the Good Trump Official
  • The Future Is Italy, and It’s Bleak
  • As a Mob Attacked the Capitol, Trump Stood By
  • Mossad Sheds New Light on Argentina Terrorist Attacks in 1990s
  • Attack on Base in Mali Brings Conflict to Doorstep of Capital
  • ​South Korea’s New President Calls for Criminal Investigation of Past Government
  • Sri Lankan Forces Raid Protest Camp at Center of Uprising
  • Justice Dept. Will Investigate Environmental Racism in Houston
  • Ethics Board Moves to Penalize Jeffrey Clark, Who Aided Trump in Election Plot
  • Josh Hawley cheered on Jan. 6. Then he fled.
  • Jan. 6 Hearings Invoke Patriotism to Urge Voters to Break With Trump
  • A Hidden New Threat to U.S. Elections
  • Rare Case of Polio Prompts Alarm and an Urgent Investigation in New York

  • New York City Jail Officer Charged With Killing Teenager While Off Duty
  • Man Charged With Threatening Racist Violence Against Buffalo Supermarket
  • G.O.P. Assails N.Y. Bail Laws After Suspect in Zeldin Attack Is Released
